title: Systemd can't start podman container after reboot due to deadlock on lockfile
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
I have created a systemd unit file on CentOS 8 to run an `opendistro` container as the user `podman`. The unit file (see [here](https://gist.github.com/sjpb/912034910298bea779745adbd8eb75ee)) was generated using `podman generate systemd opendistro --restart-policy always --name` but then modified to use the `--sdnotify=conman` approach described [here](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/5572#issuecomment-653574848) to avoid problems with the pidfile not being owned by root.

This initially works fine: systemd shows the `opendistro` service running ok and running `podman ps` as the podman user shows the container up. I did note that on running `systemctl stop opendistro` it claims to have failed due to the ExecStop (podman stop) command returning 143 but the container is stopped.

However if I reboot the host then service fails to startup, with systemd showing the ExecStart (`podman run`) command returning `126`. Trying `podman start opendistro` directly returns the same error code with the message:

```
Error: container 13fff305a82757d54303f4f627f9749c4e03edda29f91346b417ec90a5e191d7 and volume opendistro share lock ID 0: deadlock due to lock mismatch
```

To me this looks exactly like the error described in [this patch](https://github.com/mheon/libpod/commit/b8c9897dcd54d321614d1d0f7b0f973547126660).

Is there any way to work around this, e.g. by deleting lock files on unit stop or system shutdown?

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create `podman` user and `opendistro` volume.

2. Copy the linked unit file to `/etc/systemd/system/opendistro.service`, replacing `{{ opendistro_podman_user }}` with `podman`

3. Run `sudo systemctl star opendistro` and check container starts

4. Reboot host

5. Check `systemctl status opendistro`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
● opendistro.service - Podman container-opendistro.service
   Loaded: loaded (/etc/systemd/system/opendistro.service; enabled; vendor preset: disabled)
   Active: failed (Result: exit-code) since Fri 2021-03-19 11:45:02 UTC; 2h 7min ago
     Docs: man:podman-generate-systemd(1)
  Process: 34205 ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman rm --ignore -f opendistro (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
  Process: 34092 ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop --ignore opendistro -t 10 (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
  Process: 33295 ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --sdnotify=conmon --cgroups=no-conmon -d --replace --name opendistro --restart=no --user elasticsearch --ulimit memlock=-1:-1 --ulimit nofile=65536:65536 --volume open>
 Main PID: 33295 (code=exited, status=143)
    Tasks: 1 (limit: 822732)
   Memory: 463.1M
   CGroup: /system.slice/opendistro.service
           └─1958 /usr/bin/podman

Mar 19 11:22:38 proto-control.novalocal systemd[1]: Starting Podman container-opendistro.service...
Mar 19 11:22:38 proto-control.novalocal systemd[1]: Started Podman container-opendistro.service.
Mar 19 11:22:38 proto-control.novalocal podman[33214]: 6f2b6b78b2f06bfcb20b49dd5311587e7a1a9e0422a74081aa2f653ad96e7c57
Mar 19 11:45:02 proto-control.novalocal systemd[1]: Stopping Podman container-opendistro.service...
Mar 19 11:45:02 proto-control.novalocal podman[34092]: 6f2b6b78b2f06bfcb20b49dd5311587e7a1a9e0422a74081aa2f653ad96e7c57
Mar 19 11:45:02 proto-control.novalocal systemd[1]: opendistro.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=143/n/a
Mar 19 11:45:02 proto-control.novalocal podman[34205]: 6f2b6b78b2f06bfcb20b49dd5311587e7a1a9e0422a74081aa2f653ad96e7c57
Mar 19 11:45:02 proto-control.novalocal systemd[1]: opendistro.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.
Mar 19 11:45:02 proto-control.novalocal systemd[1]: Stopped Podman container-opendistro.service.
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
See service running.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 2.2.1
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman.x86_64  2.2.1-7.module_el8.3.0+699+d61d9c41    @appstream
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

No, Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Physical.

comments.body: Interestingly I just removed the `User=` and `Group=` parameters from the unit file and tried the "start, reboot, status" loop again and it comes up fine.
Please run `podman system renumber` to resolve the issue, and let us know if it happens again. Duplicate lock ID allocation should never happen, and is a sign of a serious issue.

So it has happened again. I removed the `RuntimeDirectory` directive from the above unit file (which was left in from previous attempts getting a PID-based approach) and I thought that had fixed it as it came back up after two reboots. However after the 3rd reboot I got 

```
Error: container f6863ad60f8294b434dc2c3dc1e6b7602cd994627e4d8f9aa585c4e1d2639aae and volume opendistro share lock ID 2: deadlock due to lock mismatch
```

Did `podman system renumber` then `systemctl start opendistro` and it started ok.

The message on the patch I mentioned above suggests that allocation of existing locks is a known bug?

The usual cause for this that we find is Podman's temporary files directory not being on a tmpfs. The default should be under `/run/user/$UID` for the user running Podman, but that directory is not created by systemd when a service using said user runs, so Podman may have fallen back to a directory in `/tmp`. Is `/tmp` a tmpfs?
`/tmp` isn't a tmpfs but `/run` is. Having said that only `/run/user/1000` exists which matches the `centos` user not the `podman` user (uid/gid 1001).

Alright, that may well be the cause. Podman is probably using `/tmp/containers-user-$UID` (or a similar path). That directory is most likely not being cleaned up after reboot. If Podman does not detect a reboot, it will not properly refresh its state, which will lead to errors like this.

You should be able to determine the exact path with `podman info --log-level=debug` - this will print out a debug message including the path:
`DEBU[0000] Using tmp dir /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp`
